channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18041] struct obd_uuid is too small for MGC"NID" uuid with IPv6
item.description:
IPv6 can vary in size up to 42 bytes in total which is way larger than obd_uuid size:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/4ae697a2-d2db-4791-b14e-014bb6e67487

Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: mount -t lustre -o user_xattr,flock fd33:3981:3213:f010:0:5254:14:c527@tcp:/lustre /mnt/lustre
LustreError: 16630:0:(mgc_request.c:1456:mgc_apply_recover_logs()) mgc: cannot find UUID by nid 'fd33:3981:3213:f010:0:5254:eb:f685@tcp': rc = -2
Lustre: 16630:0:(mgc_request.c:1635:mgc_process_recover_log()) MGCfd33:3981:3213:f010:0:5254:14:c527@tcp: error processing lustre-cliir log recovery: rc = -2
Lustre: 16630:0:(mgc_request.c:1899:mgc_process_log()) MGCfd33:3981:3213:f010:0:5254:14:c527@tcp: IR log lustre-cliir failed, not fatal: rc = -2
LustreError: lustre-MDT0000-mdc-ff12b8ef43b85800: operation mds_connect to node fd33:3981:3213:f010:0:5254:14:c527@tcp failed: rc = -11

Update places in the kernel where obd_uuid is used for MGS.
item.environment: MGS configured with IPv6 strings that can't be compressed into UUID_MAX size.
item.summary: struct obd_uuid is too small for MGC"NID" uuid with IPv6

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment: review-dne-ipv6 test sessions on master branch keep failing with this failure.

conf-sanity test 98 also hit the same issue:

[21831.321862] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: mount -t lustre -o user_xattr,flock fd33:3981:3213:f020:0:5254:25:16ab@tcp:/lustre /mnt/lustre
[21832.391801] LustreError: 1256214:0:(mgc_request.c:1459:mgc_apply_recover_logs()) mgc: cannot find UUID by nid 'fd33:3981:3213:f020:0:5254:d0:c68@tcp': rc = -2
[21832.394211] Lustre: 1256214:0:(mgc_request.c:1640:mgc_process_recover_log()) MGCfd33:3981:3213:f020:0:5254:25:16ab@tcp: error processing lustre-cliir log recovery: rc = -2
[21832.396726] Lustre: 1256214:0:(mgc_request.c:1910:mgc_process_log()) MGCfd33:3981:3213:f020:0:5254:25:16ab@tcp: IR log lustre-cliir failed, not fatal: rc = -2
[21851.894139] LustreError: lustre-MDT0003-mdc-ffff8fb14437c000: operation mds_connect to node fd33:3981:3213:f020:0:5254:d0:c68@tcp failed: rc = -11
[21882.613986] LustreError: lustre-MDT0003-mdc-ffff8fb14437c000: operation mds_connect to node fd33:3981:3213:f020:0:5254:d0:c68@tcp failed: rc = -11

https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/81353f69-7ff3-46d7-9aa7-c10f4d61b625

sanity-sec test 31 failed consistently in full-dne-ipv6-part-2 test sessions on master branch:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/239efd8f-cfe3-408e-a083-45a005e7d526

simmonsja, could you please look into this? It is a regular cause of IPv6 test session failures, and is preventing the review-dne-ipv6 test sessions from being enforced.

IPv6 can vary in size up to 42 bytes in total

Colin and I were discussing this, and if we remove the : from the IPv6 string and fully printed all quads of the address (no abbreviation, just a full hex string), then the address would only be 32 bytes, the same as a UUID, and will always fit. This has the benefit that the NID of the server would still be visible in the device name.

Looks like this is now being hit for review-dne-ipv6 runs since 2025-09-19, 6x TIMEOUTs in the past 4 days and never any similar failures in the past 6 months:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/search?horizon=15552000&status%5B%5D=TIMEOUT&test_groups%5B%5D=review-dne-ipv6&test_set_script_id=5e9346a2-09e0-11e9-a2cc-52540065bddc&source=test_sets#redirect

This appears to be different from LU-19360 which is failing during initial OST setup, while this appears to be hit during client mount, so it smells like progress.

full-dne-ipv6-part-3 test session initialization also failed with the same issue:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/4dcd390c-5605-4865-b860-739975b6990e

sanity-sec test 31 failed with the same issue in full-dne-ipv6-part-2 test session:

[22837.865416] LNet: Added LNI fd33:3981:3213:f010:0:5254:c3:2a8c@tcp999 [8/256/0/180]
[22837.871541] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: mkdir -p /mnt/lustre
[22837.881550] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: mount -t lustre -o user_xattr,flock,network=tcp999 fd33:3981:3213:f010:0:5254:76:562b@tcp,fd33:3981:3213:f010:0:5254:76:562b@tcp999:/lustre /mnt/lustre
[22838.912921] LustreError: 1795392:0:(mgc_request.c:1456:mgc_apply_recover_logs()) mgc: cannot find UUID by nid 'fd33:3981:3213:f010:0:5254:0:b6a7@tcp': rc = -2
[22838.913130] Lustre: 1795392:0:(mgc_request.c:1635:mgc_process_recover_log()) MGCfd33:3981:3213:f010:0:5254:76:562b@tcp: error processing lustre-cliir log recovery: rc = -2
[22838.913343] Lustre: 1795392:0:(mgc_request.c:1899:mgc_process_log()) MGCfd33:3981:3213:f010:0:5254:76:562b@tcp: IR log lustre-cliir failed, not fatal: rc = -2
[22858.415938] LustreError: lustre-MDT0003-mdc-ff449e15dfb4a800: operation mds_connect to node fd33:3981:3213:f010:0:5254:0:b6a7@tcp999 failed: rc = -11

https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/98cf4f27-ba2b-4cba-9d5f-47e4bde4eaf6
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/169f4357-56df-4207-b5ab-6200d2d48160
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/e6888585-762c-4d3e-ad3d-cded63cb93a8

"James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58599
Subject: LU-18041 msg: use UUIDs instead of NIDs for client setup
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 5e47d2da71aff76e0782bbc8682e86c8689a687b

"James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55878
Subject: LU-18041 obd: debug uuid <-> NID handling
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 4c4224c72cb729f983e6810e90e2a95db4df9db9

https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55818]

More patches are needed besides this.
